---
title: "Yaboot with OSX+Ubuntu+Kubuntu?"
date: 2005-03-31
forum: Apple PPC Users
---

### Post by Leonida on 2005-03-31
Yes, I'm becoming an ubuntu addicted :)
I've 3 partitions: one with OSX, one with Ubuntu and one with Kubuntu.
Yaboot works fine with dualboot, but how configure it with 3?
Here are my two yaboot.conf. How to merge?

[SIZE=4]**Ubuntu**[/SIZE]
```
## yaboot.conf generated by the Ubuntu installer
##
## run: "man yaboot.conf" for details. Do not make changes until you have!!
## see also: /usr/share/doc/yaboot/examples for example configurations.
##
## For a dual-boot menu, add one or more of:
## bsd=/dev/hdaX, macos=/dev/hdaY, macosx=/dev/hdaZ

boot=/dev/hda2
device=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:
partition=6
root=/dev/hda6
timeout=100
defaultos=macosx
install=/usr/lib/yaboot/yaboot
magicboot=/usr/lib/yaboot/ofboot
enablecdboot
macosx=/dev/hda5

image=/boot/vmlinux
	label=Linux
	read-only
	initrd=/boot/initrd.img
	append="quiet splash"

image=/boot/vmlinux.old
	label=old
	read-only
	initrd=/boot/initrd.img.old
	append="quiet splash"
```

[SIZE=4]**Kubuntu**[/SIZE]
```
## yaboot.conf generated by the Ubuntu installer
##
## run: "man yaboot.conf" for details. Do not make changes until you have!!
## see also: /usr/share/doc/yaboot/examples for example configurations.
##
## For a dual-boot menu, add one or more of:
## bsd=/dev/hdaX, macos=/dev/hdaY, macosx=/dev/hdaZ

boot=/dev/hda2
device=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:
partition=4
root=/dev/hda4

timeout=100
install=/usr/lib/yaboot/yaboot
magicboot=/usr/lib/yaboot/ofboot
enablecdboot
macosx=/dev/hda5

image=/boot/vmlinux
	label=Linux
	read-only
	initrd=/boot/initrd.img
	append="quiet splash"

image=/boot/vmlinux.old
	label=old
	read-only
	initrd=/boot/initrd.img.old
	append="quiet splash"

# This entry automatically added by the Ubuntu installer for an existing
# Linux installation on /dev/hda6.
image=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6,/boot/vmlinux
    label=hda6-Linux
    root=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6
    append="root=/dev/hda6 ro quiet splash"
    initrd=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6,/boot/initrd.img

# This entry automatically added by the Ubuntu installer for an existing
# Linux installation on /dev/hda6.
image=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6,/boot/vmlinux.old
    label=hda6-old
    root=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6
    append="root=/dev/hda6 ro quiet splash"
    initrd=/pci@f4000000/ata-6@d/disk@0:6,/boot/initrd.img.old
```

---

### Post by godsunderstudysdad on 2005-03-31
:?: You don't. At least, we don't. We have the Yaboot partition, one for OSX and one for Ubuntu.
Current (Hoary) Ubuntu gives you a very good graphical loading screen where you can choose to run either a Gnome or a  KDE session.
